intro: Benstonea humilis, also known as Benstonea, is a plant species native to the tropical rainforests of Papua New Guinea, belonging to the family Pandanaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Benstonea humilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Benstonea is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health issues in indigenous communities.
